lines.english: The Guru instructs His wandering Sikhs;

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸੁ ਗੁਰ ਸੇਵਿ ਸਦਾ ਦਿਨੁ ਰਾਤੀ ਦੁਖ ਭੰਜਨ ਸੰਗਿ ਸਖਾਤਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੩॥
lines.translit: tis gur sev sadaa din raatee dukh bhanjan sang sakhaataa he |13|
lines.english: So serve the Guru, forever, day and night; He is the Destroyer of pain - He is with you as your companion. ||13||
if they go astray, He sets them on the right path.
Even Brahma, Indra and Shiva do not know it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਭਗਤਿ ਕਰਹਿ ਕਿਆ ਪ੍ਰਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: gur kee bhagat kareh kiaa praanee |
lines.english: O mortal being, what devotional worship have you performed to the Guru?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਅਲਖੁ ਕਹਹੁ ਕਿਉ ਲਖੀਐ ਜਿਸੁ ਬਖਸੇ ਤਿਸਹਿ ਪਛਾਤਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੪॥
lines.translit: satigur alakh kahahu kiau lakheeai jis bakhase tiseh pachhaataa he |14|
lines.english: Tell me, how can the unknowable True Guru be known? He alone attains this realization, whom the Lord forgives. ||14||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਨਿਰਮਲ ਜੋਤਿ ਸਬਾਈ ਘਟਿ ਦੀਪਕੁ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਜਾਤਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੫॥
lines.translit: ahinis niramal jot sabaaee ghatt deepak guramukh jaataa he |15|
lines.english: Day and night, the Gurmukh sees the immaculate Divine Light everywhere; this lamp illuminates his heart. ||15||
One who enshrines love for the Word of the Guru's Bani, meets with Him.
One who has love within, obtains the Blessed Vision of His Darshan.
Whoever tastes it, sees the Blessed Vision of the Lord's Darshan.
The food of spiritual wisdom is the supremely sweet essence.
Those who serve the True Guru are supreme and famous.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਰਸਨੁ ਦੇਖਿ ਮਿਲੇ ਬੈਰਾਗੀ ਮਨੁ ਮਨਸਾ ਮਾਰਿ ਸਮਾਤਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੬॥
lines.translit: darasan dekh mile bairaagee man manasaa maar samaataa he |16|
lines.english: Beholding His Darshan, the unattached one meets the Lord; subduing the mind's desires, he merges into the Lord. ||16||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਹਰਿ ਜਸੁ ਹਰਿ ਜਨ ਕੀ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਦੀਜੈ ਜਿਨ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਹਰਿ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਜਾਤਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੭॥੫॥੧੧॥
lines.translit: naanak har jas har jan kee sangat deejai jin satigur har prabh jaataa he |17|5|11|
lines.english: Please bless Nanak with the Lord's Praises, and the Sangat, the Congregation of the Lord's humble servants; through the True Guru, they know their Lord God. ||17||5||11||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨ ਘਟ ਘਟ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੁ ਪਛਾਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: tin ghatt ghatt antar braham pachhaanaa |
lines.english: Deep within each and every heart, they recognize God.
He established and contemplates the worldly sphere.
Maaroo, First Mehl:
The True Lord is the Creator of the Universe.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਕਰਤਾ ਕਰਿ ਕਰਿ ਵੇਖੈ ਸਾਚਾ ਵੇਪਰਵਾਹਾ ਹੇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: aape karataa kar kar vekhai saachaa veparavaahaa he |1|
lines.english: He Himself created the creation, and beholds it; He is True and independent. ||1||
The two travellers have set out in two directions.
He created the beings of different kinds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪੂਰੇ ਵਿਣੁ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਨ ਹੋਈ ਸਚੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਜਪਿ ਲਾਹਾ ਹੇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: gur poore vin mukat na hoee sach naam jap laahaa he |2|
lines.english: Without the Perfect Guru, no one is liberated. Chanting the True Name, one profits. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੜਹਿ ਮਨਮੁਖ ਪਰੁ ਬਿਧਿ ਨਹੀ ਜਾਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: parreh manamukh par bidh nahee jaanaa |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukhs read and study, but they do not know the way.
They do not understand the Naam, the Name of the Lord; they wander, deluded by doubt.

lines.gurmukhi: ਲੈ ਕੈ ਵਢੀ ਦੇਨਿ ਉਗਾਹੀ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਕਾ ਗਲਿ ਫਾਹਾ ਹੇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: lai kai vadtee den ugaahee duramat kaa gal faahaa he |3|
lines.english: They take bribes, and give false testimony; the noose of evil-mindedness is around their necks. ||3||
They read the Simritees, the Shaastras and the Puraanas;

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਣੁ ਗੁਰ ਪੂਰੇ ਤਤੁ ਨ ਪਾਈਐ ਸਚ ਸੂਚੇ ਸਚੁ ਰਾਹਾ ਹੇ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: vin gur poore tat na paaeeai sach sooche sach raahaa he |4|
lines.english: Without the Perfect Guru, the essence of reality is not obtained. The true and pure beings walk the Path of Truth. ||4||
they argue and debate, but do not know the essence of reality.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਕਉ ਨਦਰਿ ਕਰੇ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਅਪਨੀ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਬਦੁ ਸਲਾਹਾ ਹੇ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: jin kau nadar kare prabh apanee guramukh sabad salaahaa he |5|
lines.english: Those whom God blesses with His Glance of Grace become Gurmukh, and praise the Word of the Shabad. ||5||
All praise God and listen, and listen and speak.
He Himself is wise, and He Himself judges the Truth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਣਿ ਕਹੀਐ ਕੋ ਅੰਤੁ ਨ ਜਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: sun kaheeai ko ant na jaanee |
lines.english: Listening and speaking, no one knows His limits.
Many listen and listen, and speak the Guru's Bani.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਕਉ ਅਲਖੁ ਲਖਾਏ ਆਪੇ ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ ਬੁਧਿ ਤਾਹਾ ਹੇ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: jaa kau alakh lakhaae aape akath kathaa budh taahaa he |6|
lines.english: He alone is wise, unto whom the unseen Lord reveals Himself; he speaks the Unspoken Speech. ||6||
the ignorant sing songs of joy.
At birth, the congratulations pour in;

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਜਨਮੈ ਤਿਸੁ ਸਰਪਰ ਮਰਣਾ ਕਿਰਤੁ ਪਇਆ ਸਿਰਿ ਸਾਹਾ ਹੇ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: jo janamai tis sarapar maranaa kirat peaa sir saahaa he |7|
lines.english: Whoever is born, is sure to die, according to the destiny of past deeds inscribed upon his head by the Sovereign Lord King. ||7||
Union and separation were created by my God.
Creating the Universe, He gave it pain and pleasure.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਖ ਸੁਖ ਹੀ ਤੇ ਭਏ ਨਿਰਾਲੇ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੀਲੁ ਸਨਾਹਾ ਹੇ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: dukh sukh hee te bhe niraale guramukh seel sanaahaa he |8|
lines.english: The Gurmukhs remain unaffected by pain and pleasure; they wear the armor of humility. ||8||
They purchase the true merchandise, contemplating the Guru.
The noble people are traders in Truth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚਾ ਵਖਰੁ ਜਿਸੁ ਧਨੁ ਪਲੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਸਚੈ ਓਮਾਹਾ ਹੇ ॥੯॥
lines.translit: sachaa vakhar jis dhan palai sabad sachai omaahaa he |9|
lines.english: One who has the wealth of the true commodity in his lap, is blessed with the rapture of the True Shabad. ||9||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂੰਜੀ ਸਾਬਤੁ ਰਾਸਿ ਸਲਾਮਤਿ ਚੂਕਾ ਜਮ ਕਾ ਫਾਹਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: poonjee saabat raas salaamat chookaa jam kaa faahaa he |10|
lines.english: His stock is safe, and his capital is safe and sound. The noose of Death is cut away from around his neck. ||10||
The false dealings lead only to loss.
The trades of the Gurmukh are pleasing to God.
